description: Increased risk of severe bleeding.
May reduce the blood pressure-lowering effect of Lisinopril.
Omeprazole may reduce the effectiveness of Clopidogrel in preventing heart attacks.
Increased risk of muscle damage (myopathy/rhabdomyolysis).
Risk of lactic acidosis; stop Metformin before imaging procedures.
Increased risk of liver damage.
Ciprofloxacin can increase Theophylline levels, leading to toxicity.
Increased risk of serotonin syndrome and seizures.
Calcium can decrease the absorption of Levothyroxine. Take them at least 4 hours apart.
Verapamil can increase Digoxin levels, leading to toxicity.
Severe, potentially fatal drop in blood pressure.
Risk of life-threatening hyperkalemia (high potassium).
Propranolol blocks the bronchodilator effect of Albuterol, potentially triggering asthma.
Increased risk of Lithium toxicity due to decreased renal clearance.
Grapefruit juice increases statin levels, raising the risk of muscle side effects.
Increased risk of Methotrexate toxicity (bone marrow suppression).
Increased risk of irregular heart rhythms (QT prolongation).
They may compete for the same binding site, reducing effectiveness.
Phenytoin decreases the effectiveness of oral contraceptives.
Iron decreases the absorption of Doxycycline. Separate doses by 2 hours.
